26: 27: Wagtail is an open source content management system built on Django, with a strong community and commercial support. It's focused on user experience, and offers precise control for designers and developers. 28: 29: ![Wagtail screenshot](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wagtail/wagtail@main/.github/wagtail-screenshot-with-browser.png) 30: 31: ### 🔥 Features 32: 33: - A fast, attractive interface for authors 34: - Complete control over front-end design and structure 35: - Scales to millions of pages and thousands of editors 36: - Fast out of the box, cache-friendly when you need it 37: - Content API for 'headless' sites with decoupled front-end 38: - Runs on a Raspberry Pi or a multi-datacenter cloud platform 39: - StreamField encourages flexible content without compromising structure 40: - Powerful, integrated search, using Elasticsearch or PostgreSQL 41: - Excellent support for images and embedded content 42: - Multi-site and multi-language ready 43: - Embraces and extends Django 44: 45: Find out more at [wagtail.org](https://wagtail.org/). 46: 47: ### 👉 Getting started 48: 49: Wagtail works with [Python 3](https://www.python.org/downloads/), on any platform. 50: 51: To get started with using Wagtail, run the following in a [virtual environment](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/venv.html): 52: 53: ![Installing Wagtail](.github/install-animation.gif) 54: 55: ```sh 56: pip install wagtail 57: wagtail start mysite 58: cd mysite 59: pip install -r requirements.txt 60: python manage.py migrate 61: python manage.py createsuperuser 62: python manage.py runserver 63: ``` 64: 65: For detailed installation and setup docs, see [the getting started tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/tutorial.html). 66: 67: ### 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Who’s using it? Add your own Wagtail site to [madewithwagtail.org](https://madewithwagtail.org). 70: 71: ### 📖 Documentation 72: 73: [docs.wagtail.org](https://docs.wagtail.org/) is the full reference for Wagtail, and includes guides for developers, designers and editors, alongside [release notes](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/) and our [roadmap](https://wagtail.org/roadmap/). 74: 75: For those who are **new to Wagtail**, the [Zen of Wagtail](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/the_zen_of_wagtail.html) will help you understand what Wagtail is, and what Wagtail is _not_. 76: 77: **For developers** who are ready to jump in to their first Wagtail website the [Getting Started Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/tutorial.html) will guide you through creating and editing your first page. 78: 79: **Do you have an existing Django project?** The [Wagtail Integration documentation](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/integrating_into_django.html) is the best place to start. 80: 81: ### 📌 Compatibility 82: 83: _(If you are reading this on GitHub, the details here may not be indicative of the current released version - please see [Compatible Django / Python versions](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/upgrading.html#compatible-django-python-versions) in the Wagtail documentation.)_ 84: 85: Wagtail supports: 86: 87: - Django 5.2.x and 6.0.x 88: - Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14 89: - PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB and SQLite (with JSON1) as database backends 90: 91: [Previous versions of Wagtail](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/upgrading.html#compatible-django-python-versions) additionally supported Python 2.7, 3.8 and earlier Django versions. 92: 93: --- 94: 95: ### 📢 Community Support 96: 97: There is an active community of Wagtail users and developers responding to questions on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/wagtail). When posting questions, please read Stack Overflow's advice on [how to ask questions](https://stackoverflow.com/help/how-to-ask) and remember to tag your question "wagtail". 98: 99: For topics and discussions that do not fit Stack Overflow's question and answer format we have a [Slack workspace](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Slack). Please respect the time and effort of volunteers by not asking the same question in multiple places. 100: 101: [![Join slack community](.github/join-slack-community.png)](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Slack) 102: 103: Our [GitHub discussion boards](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/discussions) are open for sharing ideas and plans for the Wagtail project. 104: 105: We maintain a curated list of third party packages, articles and other resources at [Awesome Wagtail](https://github.com/springload/awesome-wagtail). 106: 107: ### 🧑‍💼 Commercial Support 108: 109: Wagtail is sponsored by [Torchbox](https://torchbox.com/). See also [madewithwagtail.org/developers/](https://madewithwagtail.org/developers/) for expert Wagtail developers around the world. 110: 111: ### 🔐 Security 112: 113: We take the security of Wagtail, and related packages we maintain, seriously. If you have found a security issue with any of our projects please email us at [security@wagtail.org](mailto:security@wagtail.org) so we can work together to find and patch the issue. 593:     def _slug_is_available(slug, parent_page, page=None):
594:         """
595:         Determine whether the given slug is available for use on a child page of
596:         parent_page. If 'page' is passed, the slug is intended for use on that page
597:         (and so it will be excluded from the duplicate check).

604:         if page:
